harnesses.securityPosture: gateway callers are trusted; Nostra Cortex use requires WorkRouter ceilings and profile guardrails
harnesses.observability: profile logs, sessions, gateway status, and local artifacts
harnesses.performanceNotes: Useful near-term bootstrap harness for profile-scoped messenger interaction; project-specific throughput remains unbenchmarked.
risks: broad terminal tooling must not bypass WorkRouter
profile convenience state is not governed authority
gateway transport must not imply code mutation authority
harnesses.recommendation: Use as near-term DispatchTransport bridge and profile substrate while keeping WorkRouter as the project authority layer.

harnesses.performanceNotes: Candidate harness retained for comparison; no local authority claim.
harnesses.recommendation: Track as a candidate only until source, runtime, and guardrail behavior are validated.

harnesses.performanceNotes: Previously considered as a sidecar or profile broker pattern source; not workflow authority.
risks: subscription or provider brokerage must not become runtime authority
harnesses.recommendation: Keep as an explicit candidate for provider-broker comparison only.

harnesses.performanceNotes: Candidate harness retained for long-term comparison; no local validation yet.
harnesses.recommendation: Track as a candidate only until concrete source and capability evidence is available.

harnesses.securityPosture: D0-D1 only; no source mutation, runtime mutation, commit, push, deploy, canister call, or graph mutation
harnesses.observability: validated run artifacts, dispatch envelopes, receipts, unknown-response logs, and handoffs
harnesses.performanceNotes: Project-native governance router; throughput is currently contract-validated rather than live-benchmarked.
risks: D2 mutation lane must remain disabled until separately governed
local runtime logs are not durable governed evidence
harnesses.recommendation: Use as the stable governance boundary for D0-D1 dispatch and harness-neutral evaluation.

harnesses.executionModel: Rust worker candidate for Hetzner Phase 6 and future Cortex-native migration
harnesses.securityPosture: passive preflight only until production identity and VPS authority validation pass
harnesses.observability: build/preflight evidence and future operator-only agent run surfaces
harnesses.performanceNotes: Runtime candidate remains blocked for live authority until host-mode validation and identity enforcement are proven.
risks: must not overstate passive build/preflight as live runtime readiness
must not expose operator-only provider or runtime topology through agent-facing paths
harnesses.recommendation: Keep restricted until VPS authority, identity enforcement, and Space binding evidence are complete.

harnesses.executionModel: local operator-controlled implementation lane with explicit approvals and repo preflight
harnesses.securityPosture: source mutation authority exists only through explicit operator-governed coding sessions, not through WorkRouter or messenger adapters
harnesses.observability: git diff, test outputs, preflight checks, and final closeout evidence
harnesses.performanceNotes: Current source mutation lane; not available to WorkRouter, Hermes Gateway, Telegram, or autonomous runtime agents.
risks: must not be confused with an unattended messenger-driven executor
D2+ automation requires separate governance and isolated request-worktree controls
harnesses.recommendation: Keep as the explicit code mutation authority until a separate D2 implementation lane is governed.
